anime-sol, 43.78%. this is the tight bid-ask flip wide strat and it's one of my favorite plays on a strong runner.

the setup has two phases.

phase 1: tight bid-ask. you need a strong runner with huge volume and solid narrative that's already dumped around 40% from ath. 

pool link : https://t.co/Yf52Rgoclv

that's your entry window. deploy bid-ask with a tight range, -7% to -15% depending on how confident you are in the token. the goal here is simple — let your SOL convert to tokens as price sweeps through your bins on the way down.

once your SOL is fully converted to tokens, you close the position.

phase 2: flip to token side wide. open a new position using token side only with a 100-300% range to the upside. this wide range lets you DCA in and out as the token recovers. 

you're now sitting with tokens, collecting fees as the price moves back up through your range.

why not just use a normal wide range from the start? because on a strong runner with good volume the TVL gets absolutely crowded. 

tight range on the way down gets you in efficiently before the pool thickens. flipping to wide on token side gives you the recovery play without competing with everyone else for the same bins.

the whole thing only works if the narrative is genuinely strong. if the token is just a one-day wonder this falls apart at phase 2. pick the right token and the recovery almost always comes.

Did you know Seeker users are getting 2.5% of the @xplaceapp airdrop? 📱🪂

But how do you actually qualify?

Here’s a simple step-by-step guide to get eligible & maximize your allocation 👇

✅ Step 1: Install Correctly

→ Download @xplaceapp via the Solana dApp Store
→ This is what flags you as “eligible”
→ Web/Play Store likely won’t count for the Seeker pool

✅ Step 2: Set Up + Initial Boost

→ Sign up + connect wallet (Seed Vault)
→ Use a referral (≈500 XP bonus)
→ Complete KYC (required for card + full features)

✅ Step 3: Upgrade Once 

→ Seeker = 20% discount on memberships
→ Higher tiers = higher XP rates
→ Example: Basic: ~1% XP / Platinum: up to ~10% XP
→ This is where most of your airdrop weight comes from

✅ Step 4: Actually Use It

→ This is not a passive airdrop, you need activity
→ Deposit collateral (earn yield + XP)
→ Spend via Credit Mode (XP + USDC cashback)
→ Use it daily - consistency compounds XP fastest

✅ Step 5: Referrals = Hidden Edge

→ Earn ~40% of referral membership in USDC
→ Get bonus XP tied to their activity
→ This can outperform your own usage if done right

✅ Step 6: Stay Active Until Snapshot

→ XP = your airdrop score
→ The 2.5% pool is shared pro-rata among Seekers
→ More XP = bigger slice

✅ What most people miss

→ It’s a separate allocation pool (not just a boost)
→ Only Seeker users via the dApp Store qualify
→ Early users have a massive advantage
→ You’re still earning from the main pool
→ But Seeker users tap into an extra 2.5% pool on top
→ Same XP → eligible across 2 pools

USDC+ : it’s Reflect’s flagship version of USDC, producing interest rates with no lockups. it works by supplying USDC to overcollateralised lending pools, where borrowers pay interest to access liquidity. 

background: the team won Solana’s hackathon, Colosseum, in 2024 and has raised $3.75M led by a16z accelerator. 

partnerships & integrations: so far the team has onboarded Umbra to issue their yield-generating dollars, as well as secured partnerships with Moto & Titan.

Tiagogo explains his current strategy and why he sticks to wide pools

“My current strategy, I want to say maybe the one I use the most is very similar to the EvilPanda strategy. Just playing very wide. I'm always the wide guy. I always open minimum 90% and usually works for me. A big dump and then recovering a little bit of a rebounce and then you end up in profits.”

“Recently, you know, the meta is changing like every month. And now March has been drier than January, February. But I think something that's been playing also in the French community last week has been more quick in and out plays so quick in and out plays but also using spot strategy classic SOL only, not buying any token and just staying for five minutes.”

“But then you really have to find the volume play. We've been using a tool also that a guy has developed just to find the good pools. And we pray every time that these plays happen that we have a 5% or 10% pool because we've been really frustrated by the 1% or the 2% pool when this volume plays on 100K shitters that are there and you see that a guy has opened the 0.8 or 1% pool, we're like, come on, man. trashcan-sol, twice. $145 in 1 minute 8 seconds. $83 in 2 minutes 12 seconds. not my usual play but this volume was too good to ignore.

the setup: single side sol, 1 bin only. that's it.

but before anything else you need the right pool. this only works on 100/5 or 125/10 pools. anything with a 2% base fee won't give you enough to make this worthwhile.

the mechanic is simple. when your 1 bin fills and then fills back out, you're capturing roughly 2x the base fee in that single movement. on a 5% base fee pool that's around 10%, on a 10% pool you're looking at 20%+ in a single fill cycle. that's what the cards show.

volume needs to be in the 100k-200k per 5 minutes range for the bin to cycle fast enough. below that and you're just sitting there waiting.

the catch: you need to be completely locked in. eyes on the chart the whole time. when the bin fills you need to know immediately, and when it fills back out you're done, close and move on. this is not a set-and-forget play. one distraction and you miss the exit.

also prepare your mental before trying this. watching a single bin move in real time while $100+ is sitting in it is a different experience from holding a normal range position.

$228 in under 4 minutes total. gut strat for the right market conditions.
